name: CMS: Zero-Trust for AI
description: AI tools that can make outbound requests are constrained by data minimization and network segmentation to contain the blast radius.
rationale: CMS Zero-Trust Architecture for AI: perform threat modeling, segment networks, and apply data minimization so an AI tool accesses only the resources strictly necessary.

examples.passes: the CMS AI rule's evidence is present

source: CMS Technical Reference Architecture (TRA) — Artificial Intelligence Guidance (Business Rules BR-AI-1..6 and recommended practices; references OMB M-25-21/M-25-22, NIST AI RMF, HHS AI policy). https://www.cms.gov/tra/Foundation/FD_0080_Foundation_AI_Guidance.htm
